title: 10 best herbal creams for heartburn

remedy_properties: Digestive Enzyme Stimulant: Ginger's ability to stimulate digestive enzymes helps to break down food more efficiently, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Anti-Inflammatory Compound: Ginger's anti-inflammatory compound, gingerol, helps to reduce swelling in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of heartburn by decreasing acid production.

Antioxidant Properties: Ginger's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, reducing the severity of heartburn episodes.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, creams helps with heartburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe digestive issues. The ginger compounds, such as gingerol and shogaol, work to reduce inflammation and ease stomach discomfort. This natural remedy helps to relax the lower esophageal sphincter, preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus, thereby providing relief from heartburn and other digestive symptoms associated with acid reflux. It also aids in improving digestion and reducing symptoms of bloating.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These phenolic compounds help with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Shogaols: Similar to gingerols, shogaols are also phenolic compounds that help with heartburn by relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.
Zingiberene: A terpene, zingiberene helps with heartburn by reducing inflammation and slowing down gastric emptying, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Roots: Ginger roots are rich in gingerol, a natural compound that can help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues associated with heartburn.
Buds: Ginger buds contain a higher concentration of shogaol, a compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and can help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 pound of fresh zingiber officinale roots in the early morning to ensure highest essential oil content.
Clean and peel 1 pound of zingiber officinale roots under cold running water to remove dirt and impurities.
Dry the peeled roots in a warm oven at 150 degrees fahrenheit for 3 to 4 hours to remove excess moisture.
Grind 2 tablespoons of dried zingiber officinale root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder to increase surface area.
Mix 2 tablespoons of zingiber officinale powder with 8 ounces of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Aloe vera contains anti-inflammatory agents like aloin and aloe-emodin, which soothe and calm the esophageal mucosa, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.

Gel-forming mucilage: The thick, protective gel of aloe vera forms a barrier on the surface of the esophagus, providing a cushion against stomach acid and other digestive irritants that can trigger heartburn symptoms.

Antioxidant properties: Aloe vera's antioxidant compounds help protect the esophageal tissue from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate inflammation and contribute to chronic heartburn issues.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with heartburn because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe plant has been used for centuries to calm digestive issues, including heartburn. It works by neutralizing stomach acid and reducing inflammation in the esophagus, providing quick relief from burning sensations. Additionally, aloe vera creams can help to heal and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, promoting a healthy digestive tract and reducing the frequency of heartburn episodes.
remedy_constituents: Acemannan: Acemannan is a polysaccharide that helps with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and promoting the healing of damaged mucosal tissues.

Glucomannan: Glucomannan, a type of soluble fiber, helps with heartburn by slowing down digestion and reducing the passage of stomach acid into the esophagus, thereby providing relief from heartburn symptoms.

Alloemodin: Alloemodin is a phenolic compound that helps with heartburn by reducing the production of stomach acid and improving the healing of the esophageal lining, thus alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They contain soothing compounds like aloe-emodin, which helps to reduce inflammation and provide relief from heartburn symptoms.

Gel (inside the leaves): Although not listed, the inner gel is the most used part of the Aloe vera plant. However, since the gel is inside the leaves, the leaves are the main component. The gel contains soothing compounds that help to calm the esophagus and reduce heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1/4 cup of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax.
Melt 2 tablespoons of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax in a double boiler for 10 minutes.
Add 1 cup of aloe vera gel to the melted mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Whip the mixture for 5 minutes until it becomes thick and creamy using an electric mixer.
Pour the herbal cream into a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks.

remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Peppermint cream helps neutralize stomach acid, providing immediate relief from heartburn symptoms by reducing inflammation and soothing the esophagus.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Peppermint's active compound, menthol, reduces inflammation in the esophageal lining, which can contribute to heartburn, and also helps reduce irritation and discomfort.

Gastroprotective properties: Peppermint cream's mucilages (gum-like substances) create a protective barrier on the stomach lining, preventing acid reflux and reducing the likelihood of further damage from acidic digestive juices.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties and ability to relax the muscles in the esophagus. The menthol in peppermint creams triggers a cooling sensation that helps to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of heartburn. Additionally, peppermint creams can help to improve digestion and reduce symptoms of acid reflux, providing quick relief from heartburn discomfort. Its natural benefits make peppermint creams a popular remedy for heartburn sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol helps with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and relaxing the lower esophageal sphincter, which can help alleviate symptoms of acid reflux.
Menthone: Menthone has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing spasms and contractions that can contribute to heartburn, and also helps to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract.

Limonene: Limonene has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms, as well as helping to relax the digestive system and improve digestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Leaves are the most commonly used part of Mentha x piperita in creams for heartburn because of their high essential oil content, particularly menthol and menthone, which provide anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Stems: Stems are used due to their ability to contribute to the cream's consistency and stability, while also providing additional menthol and menthone content.
Roots: Roots are used as they contain a higher concentration of menthol and other compounds than leaves, making them a valuable addition to heartburn creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh m x piperita leaves and flowers at dawn when oil content is highest for best results.
Dry the harvested leaves and flowers in a single layer at room temperature for 2 weeks to reduce moisture.
Steep 1/2 cup of dried m x piperita in 2 cups of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids then filter the oil through a coffee filter.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1/2 tablespoon of vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Antacid and Anti-inflammatory Properties: Fennel cream's antacid properties help neutralize stomach acid, while its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by heartburn.

Carminative and Digestive Enzyme-Stimulating Effects: Fennel cream's carminative properties help expel gas and bloating from the digestive system, while its ability to stimulate digestive enzymes helps break down food more efficiently, reducing symptoms of indigestion and heartburn.

Anxiolytic (Anxiety-Reducing) Properties: Fennel cream's anxiolytic effects can help calm stomach muscles and reduce muscle spasms associated with stress and anxiety, which can contribute to or exacerbate heartburn symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare, also known as fennel, creams helps with heartburn because they contain active compounds such as anethole and fenchone, which have natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to soothe and calm the digestive system, reducing inflammation and spasms in the esophagus and stomach that can contribute to heartburn. Additionally, fennel's carminative properties help to alleviate gas and bloating, further reducing symptoms of heartburn and promoting overall digestive health. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate heartburn and other digestive issues.
remedy_constituents: Anethole: Anethole has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help relax the muscles in the esophagus and reduce inflammation that can contribute to heartburn.
Foeniculin: Foeniculin has been shown to have gastroprotective effects, which can help protect the stomach lining from acid damage and reduce the risk of heartburn.

Phenolic compounds (e.g., ferulic acid): Phenolic compounds in fennel have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the stomach and esophagus, potentially alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Rich in compounds like fenchone and estragole, which have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe heartburn and digestive issues.
Leaves: Containing volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which exhibit carminative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties that aid in relieving heartburn symptoms.

Fruits (Fenugreek seeds are often referred to as the fruits of Foeniculum vulgare): Known for their prebiotic properties, which help regulate gut bacteria, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of heartburn and digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest foeniculum vulgare roots and stems by digging up the plant when the leaves start to turn yellowish brown around september to november.
Dry the harvested foeniculum vulgare plant parts by spreading them out in a single layer on paper bags at room temperature for 7 to 10 days.
Create an herbal infusion by steeping 2 cups of dried foeniculum vulgare in 4 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes then straining.
Mix the herbal infusion with 1 cup of distilled water and 1/4 cup of vegetable glycerin to create a liquid base for the cream.
Combine the liquid base with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of shea butter then heat the mixture in a double boiler until the wax and butter are fully incorporated.

remedy_properties: Allantoin: Allantoin helps to soothe and calm the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn. Its anti-inflammatory properties also reduce swelling and pain in the esophagus.

Rutin: Rutin is a flavonoid that has been shown to strengthen blood vessels and improve circulation, which can help to reduce symptoms of acid reflux and heartburn by improving the flow of digestive enzymes and reducing inflammation in the digestive tract.

Allergicum: Allergicum is a component of comfrey that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which helps to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Symphytum officinale, also known as comfrey, creams helps with heartburn because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to soothe the digestive tract. The cream contains allantoin, a compound that promotes tissue repair and reduces inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining. This helps to alleviate symptoms of heartburn, such as acid reflux and discomfort. The anti-inflammatory effects of comfrey cream also reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, providing quick relief from heartburn and promoting a faster recovery.
remedy_constituents: Tannins: They help with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms.
Flavonoids: They help with heartburn by possessing anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that can soothe and protect the digestive tract lining.
Allantoin: It helps with heartburn by acting as a demulcent, forming a protective barrier on the mucous membranes of the esophagus and stomach to prevent irritation and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Their mucilaginous properties help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn.
Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, they may help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Buds: They contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that may help protect the esophageal mucosa and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 100 grams of dried symphytum officinale in 500 milliliters of carrier oil for six hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids for better clarity.
Mix 10 percent of beeswax with 10 percent of shea butter in a double boiler.
Combine the infused oil mixture with the beeswax and shea butter mixture in a heat-proof container.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken before transferring it to a clean container for storage.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can exacerbate heartburn symptoms and damage to the esophageal lining.

Soothing and calming effects: Chamomile's calming and soothing properties can help relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES) muscle, which can become weakened or damaged due to GERD (Gastroesophageal Reflux Disease), reducing symptoms of heartburn and regurgitation.

Seeds: They contain apigenin, an antioxidant that helps to reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Leaves: They possess flavonoids and terpenoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing heartburn and digestive discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100 grams of fresh matricaria chamomilla flowers or 20 grams of dried flowers to use in your cream.
Steep the flowers in 200ml of hot water for 10 minutes to create a flower infusion.
Combine the cooled flower infusion with 100 grams of beeswax and 100ml of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 30 minutes to dissolve the beeswax and blend the ingredients together.
Strain the mixture and whip it with 50ml of aloe vera gel to create a smooth and creamy texture.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The leaves contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 30g of dried tilia platyphyllos flowers in mid-summer when the buds are in full bloom.
Infuse the flowers in 100ml of hot water for 5-7 minutes to create a strong infusion.
Strain the infusion and mix it with 20g of beeswax and 20ml of jojoba oil.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler at 60°c for 10-15 minutes to melt the beeswax.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and thicken for 30 minutes before applying.

remedy_properties: Mucilages: Herbal marshmallow creams contain mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from inflammation and irritation caused by heartburn.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in herbal marshmallow creams help neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate heartburn symptoms, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the digestive tract.

Soothing Saponins: The saponins in herbal marshmallow creams have a natural ability to reduce inflammation and relax the esophageal sphincter, which can help alleviate heartburn symptoms by preventing stomach acid from flowing back up into the esophagus.

remedy_constituents: Mucilages: Help with heartburn by forming a protective barrier on the mucous membranes of the esophagus, reducing inflammation and soothing irritation caused by acid reflux.
Gallic acid: Help with heartburn by exhibiting anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can reduce inflammation and protect the esophageal mucosa from damage caused by acid reflux.

Althaea officinalis polysaccharides: Help with heartburn by forming a protective barrier on the mucous membranes of the esophagus, reducing inflammation and soothing irritation caused by acid reflux, and also by promoting the healing of damaged tissues.

Barks: Althaea officinalis barks contain mucilage, similar to the roots and leaves, which can be used in creams to treat heartburn by forming a protective barrier against stomach acid.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10-20 grams of dried root althaea officinalis in the summer months when the plant is in full bloom.
Grind the root into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 5 minutes.
Combine 30 grams of beeswax, 60 grams of coconut oil, and 10 grams of powder in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture for 10 minutes or until the beeswax has fully melted and the mixture reaches 160°f.
Strain the mixture into a clean container and let it cool to room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms by coating and protecting the mucous membranes.

Flavonoids: The flavonoids present in herbal licorice creams have antioxidant properties, which can help neutralize free radicals that may contribute to heartburn and other digestive issues, promoting a healthier gut environment.

Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects on the mucous membranes of the esophagus, helping to calm and protect against acid reflux, while also reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.

The main active compound, glycyrrhizin, reduces inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, providing relief from heartburn symptoms. Additionally, glycyrrhizin helps to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing acid reflux and allowing stomach contents to pass into the small intestine more efficiently, thereby alleviating heartburn and discomfort. Glycyrrhizin: This triterpenoid saponin has anti-inflammatory and mucilaginous properties, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.

Seeds: While not as commonly used as roots or leaves, seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra may also be utilized for their potential anti-inflammatory properties and to contribute to the overall effect of heartburn relief creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and clean them thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris.
Infuse the dried roots in 1 liter of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into a clean container and discard the solids.
Mix 2% of the infused oil with 98% of a moisturizing base such as beeswax and shea butter.
Whip the mixture using a hand mixer for 10 minutes or until it reaches a smooth and creamy consistency.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: Dandelion cream's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, helping to alleviate heartburn symptoms by calming irritated tissues.

The cream's active compounds, including taraxasterol and taraxeryl acetate, help to reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus, while also promoting the production of stomach mucus to protect the lining. remedy_constituents: Inulin: Inulin is a type of fructan that can help alleviate heartburn by promoting digestive health, reducing inflammation in the digestive tract, and improving gut motility.

Taraxasterol: Taraxasterol is a triterpene that has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help reduce inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach, contributing to heartburn relief.

Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which may aid in reducing inflammation and alleviating heartburn symptoms.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 50-100 flowers of taraxacum officinale, carefully selecting fresh and clean ones for use.
Combine 50g of taraxacum officinale flowers, 200g of coconut oil, and 100g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 180-190°f for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean container, discarding the solids.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2-3 hours before applying it to the skin.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle creams helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm the esophagus and stomach lining. The herb's ability to reduce inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract reduces irritation and discomfort caused by acid reflux. remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Stinging nettle's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms.
Antacid properties: The mucilage present in stinging nettle cream acts as a natural antacid, neutralizing stomach acid and providing quick relief from heartburn discomfort.

Barks: The bark of the stinging nettle plant contains flavonoids and terpenes that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather one cup of dried nettle leaves and two cups of cold-pressed coconut oil in a heat-resistant container.
Combine the dried nettle leaves with the coconut oil in a double boiler setup and slowly warm over low heat for 3 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into another heat-resistant container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the nettle leaves.
Add one tablespoon of beeswax and five drops of peppermint essential oil to the strained mixture, stirring until the beeswax is fully incorporated.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized tubes or jars, allowing it to cool and solidify at room temperature before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wormwood creams helps with heartburn because of its natural ability to soothe and calm the digestive system. The cream's active compound, thujone, has a unique property that relaxes the muscle rings in the esophagus, allowing food and acid to pass through more smoothly. Additionally, wormwood's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the stomach lining, decreasing the production of excess acid that can cause heartburn symptoms. remedy_recipe: Harvest dried artemisia absinthium leaves measure out 20 grams for every 100 milliliters of carrier oil.
Mix the dried artemisia absinthium leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place and allow it to steep for 4-6 weeks shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth into a separate container discarding the solids and squeezing the cheesecloth tightly.
Mix one teaspoon of the infused oil with three teaspoons of beeswax and heat gently until melted then pour into sterilized containers.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in elder creams help to reduce swelling and irritation in the esophageal lining, reducing discomfort and pain associated with heartburn.

Barks: Contains powerful antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as phenolic acids, which aid in protecting the esophageal mucosa from acid damage.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh sambucus nigra leaves and dry them in a cool dark place for 7-10 days.
Combine 20g of dried sambucus nigra leaves with 100ml of cold pressed olive oil in a heat-proof glass container.
Place the mixture in a slow cooker on low heat for 4 hours allowing the herbs to infuse into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar discarding the solids and storing the liquid extract.
Mix 20ml of the sambucus nigra oil extract with 80g of beeswax in a double boiler heating until the wax is fully melted.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sloe creams helps with heartburn because they contain a unique blend of natural ingredients that work together to alleviate symptoms. Anti-inflammatory properties: The antioxidants and flavonoids present in sloe cream reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, which can help alleviate heartburn pain and discomfort caused by acid reflux.

Digestive soothing properties: The herbs like marshmallow root, licorice root, or slippery elm in sloe creams have a soothing effect on the digestive system, reducing irritation and inflammation in the esophagus and stomach that can contribute to heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain compounds that help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Fruits: The fruits of the sloe plant contain anthocyanins, powerful antioxidants that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate digestive discomfort caused by heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh prunus spinosa leaves and flowers then rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine the harvested plants with coconut oil in a ratio of 1:3 and simmer for 30 minutes over low heat.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to extract the beneficial compounds from the plant material.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the extracted liquid in a ratio of 1:2 and stir until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into glass containers and allow it to cool and set at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sumach creams helps with heartburn because of its unique properties that soothe and protect the esophageal lining. The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as beta-carotene and quercetin, reduce inflammation and irritation caused by stomach acid reflux. Additionally, sumach creams' antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate heartburn symptoms. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, it provides rapid relief from discomfort and burning sensations associated with heartburn, promoting a sense of calm and well-being.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Herbal sumac creams contain antacids that help neutralize stomach acid, reducing symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sumac's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as gallic acid and quercetin, help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate discomfort associated with heartburn.

Gastric mucosa protective properties: Herbal sumac creams contain astringent and demulcent substances that help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by gastric acid and other irritants, reducing the risk of erosive esophagitis.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhyzome, a type of underground stem, contains compounds that help reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues associated with heartburn.
Roots: The roots of the sumach plant have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including heartburn, due to their ability to calm the stomach lining and reduce acidity.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry 100 grams of rhus coriaria leaves and stems in shade for 7-10 days.
Grind 50 grams of dried rhus coriaria into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 20 grams of powdered rhus coriaria with 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler at low heat.
Add 40 grams of coconut oil and 10 grams of olive oil to the mixture, stirring until fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized containers and allow it to cool and solidify for 30-40 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Wild marjoram creams helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The herb's active compounds, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, work to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach lining, which can help alleviate symptoms of heartburn. Additionally, wild marjoram has been shown to increase the production of mucus, which can act as a natural barrier against stomach acid, further reducing discomfort and burning sensations associated with heartburn. This makes it a popular natural remedy for those seeking relief from this common digestive issue.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Wild marjoram cream helps neutralize stomach acid, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn. This is because the cream contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to inhibit gastric secretion and reduce acidity.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help soothe and calm the esophagus and stomach lining, reducing redness and swelling that can exacerbate heartburn symptoms. This is due to the presence of flavonoids like luteolin, which have potent anti-inflammatory effects.

Spasmolytic properties: Wild marjoram cream's spasmolytic compounds help relax the LES (lower esophageal sphincter), reducing inflammation and spasms that can contribute to heartburn symptoms. This is achieved through the action of terpenes like camphor, which have been shown to relax smooth muscle tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain a high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective in soothing heartburn symptoms.
Barks: The barks have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including heartburn, due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum vulgare leaves and flowers in the morning after dew has evaporated for optimal potency.
Dry the plant material using a food dehydrator at 55°c for 24 hours to preserve its medicinal properties.
Combine 30g of dried origanum vulgare with 300ml of sweet almond oil in a heatproof glass container and warm it gently.
Steep the mixture for 2-3 weeks, shaking the container daily to ensure even infusion, before straining the liquid and discarding the solids.
Mix the infused oil with 50g of beeswax and 10ml of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and then pouring into sterilized jars for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme creams helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The herb's antimicrobial actions also help to combat bacteria that can contribute to acid reflux, while its antispasmodic effects alleviate stomach cramps and discomfort associated with heartburn. Additionally, thyme creams can help neutralize stomach acids and relax the lower esophageal sphincter, allowing food to pass through the digestive system more efficiently.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Thyme cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn by soothing the esophageal mucosa.

Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial properties can help combat Helicobacter pylori (H. pylori) bacteria, a common cause of heartburn and gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD), reducing bacterial load and symptoms.

Soothing demulcents: The demulcent properties of thyme cream create a protective barrier on the esophageal mucosa, shielding it from further irritation and providing relief from heartburn symptoms such as burning, pain, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Thyme roots have a high concentration of phenolic compounds, which provide anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help soothe heartburn symptoms.
Barks: Thyme barks contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that contribute to their antacid and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in alleviating heartburn discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10 grams of dried thyme flowers and 20 grams of coconut oil.
Infuse the dried thyme flowers in the coconut oil for 30 minutes at 60 degrees celsius.
Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container.
Add 5 grams of beeswax to the infused oil and heat gently until melted and well combined.
Pour the mixture into sterilized jars and allow it to cool before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinaberry creams helps with heartburn because of its unique composition that provides quick relief from acid reflux. The cream's natural ingredients, including herbal extracts such as berberis and glycyrrhizin, work together to neutralize stomach acidity and soothe the esophageal lining. This helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn, allowing for faster healing and a return to normal digestion. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties help to coat and protect the mucous membranes, providing long-lasting relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antacid properties: Chinaberry creams contain compounds like malic acid, which helps neutralize stomach acidity and provides fast relief from heartburn symptoms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Chinaberry's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids, can reduce inflammation in the esophageal lining, which is often associated with heartburn, and alleviate discomfort.

Antioxidant properties: The high antioxidant content of chinaberry creams can help protect the stomach lining from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress and potential irritation that can contribute to heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, roots are often used in traditional medicine to soothe and calm irritated esophageal tissues, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and antioxidants, seeds have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort associated with heartburn, making them a valuable component in medicinal creams.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried leaves of melia azedarach plant in sufficient quantity for the required amount of cream.
Grind 100 grams of dried leaves into fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Mix 10 grams of ground leaves with 20 grams of beeswax and 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat mixture at low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until beeswax is fully dissolved and mixture reaches 60 degrees celsius.
Allow mixture to cool and solidify at room temperature before transferring it into clean jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric creams helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relieve symptoms of acid reflux. The active compound curcumin in turmeric works by neutralizing excess stomach acid and promoting the production of mucus that protects the esophageal lining from damage. Additionally, turmeric's natural antacid properties help to reduce the frequency and severity of heartburn episodes, providing fast and effective relief for those suffering from this common condition.
remedy_properties: Curcumin: A potent anti-inflammatory compound found in turmeric, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of heartburn by soothing the esophageal lining and reducing acid production.
Cuminum amylum: A type of starch derived from cumin seeds, which can help to neutralize excess stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Gingerol: A bioactive compound found in ginger, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help to relax the LES (lower esophageal sphincter) muscle, reducing acid reflux and heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the turmeric plant contain a high concentration of curcumin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm heartburn symptoms.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of turmeric have been traditionally used to make medicinal creams for heartburn due to their ability to reduce inflammation and improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including 2 tablespoons of turmeric powder, 1 tablespoon of coconut oil and 1 tablespoon of beeswax.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler set over simmering water for approximately 5 minutes.
Add the turmeric powder to the melted coconut oil and stir until well combined and smooth taking about 2 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and slowly add the melted beeswax stirring continuously until fully incorporated within 1 minute.
Pour the mixture into a glass jar and allow it to cool and solidify at room temperature for approximately 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with heartburn because it contains the active compound centaureidin, which has a unique ability to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and relax the lower esophageal sphincter (LES). This relief from inflammation and LES relaxation can help to alleviate symptoms of heartburn, such as acid reflux and regurgitation. Additionally, the cream's anti-inflammatory properties may also help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes lining the esophagus, further reducing discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_properties: Aloe vera gel: Soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Licorice root extract: Inhibits the production of stomach acid and reduces inflammation in the digestive tract, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
Slippery elm inner bark: Forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, coating and soothing the esophagus and reducing irritation caused by heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: Containing compounds that help neutralize stomach acid, roots are effective in soothing and calming digestive issues like heartburn.
Barks: The barks contain anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Gather ingredients including dried ginkgo biloba leaves 30g, coconut oil 200ml, beeswax 20g, and vitamin e oil 10 drops.
Mix dried ginkgo biloba leaves with coconut oil in a double boiler heating the mixture for 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter removing solids and discarding residue retaining clear herbal extract.
Combine strained herbal extract with melted beeswax and vitamin e oil in a small saucepan heating gently until fully incorporated.
Pour the resulting cream into sterilized jars sealing tightly and allowing to cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before storing.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for heartburn are topical treatments that help alleviate heartburn symptoms by soothing and protecting the esophageal lining. They contain a blend of herbs, such as Zingiber officinale, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Aloe barbadensis, Foeniculum vulgare, Mentha x piperita, Althaea officinalis, Aloe vera, Cinchona officinalis, Lavandula angustifolia, and Ulmus rubra, which work together to reduce inflammation and provide relief. If left untreated, heartburn can lead to discomfort, difficulty swallowing, and even long-term damage to the esophagus and teeth, severely impacting daily life and overall well-being.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to heartburn that are treated with herbal creams are skin conditions such as eczema, acne, and rosacea. These creams often contain herbs like aloe vera, chamomile, and tea tree oil, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help calm irritated skin. Additionally, some herbal creams may be used to treat minor burns, wounds, and ulcers, providing relief from discomfort, itching, and redness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with heartburn is a blend of Aloe vera, Chamomile, and Licorice root. Aloe vera soothes and calms the esophagus, while Chamomile reduces inflammation and eases digestion. Licorice root, rich in glycyrrhizin, helps to neutralize stomach acid and protect the mucous membranes. Together, these creams provide a natural and effective solution to alleviate heartburn symptoms, promoting a smoother digestive experience and quick relief from discomfort. Regular application can lead to long-term heartburn prevention.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of alternative and complementary medicine (New York, N.Y.), herbal creams may be beneficial for heartburn due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. This study reviewed 22 studies, including 13 human studies, which found that various herbal preparations such as Myrtus communis, Cydonia oblonga, and rikkunshito alleviated symptoms related to gastroesophageal reflux disease (GERD). Specifically, Cydonia oblonga showed a marked reduction in GERD symptoms comparable to omeprazole, a common medication for heartburn, and its therapeutic effect even persisted after discontinuation of the drug.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with heartburn because they contain the active compound gingerol, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. Ginger has been traditionally used to soothe digestive issues and alleviate symptoms of heartburn, such as bloating and discomfort. The cream's topical application allows the active ingredients to directly target the affected area, providing quick relief from heartburn. Additionally, the warming and calming properties of ginger may also help to relax the muscles in the digestive tract, further reducing heartburn symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Shogaols: Shogaols have a similar effect to gingerols, with additional analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system and provide relief from heartburn.
Volatiles (specifically, bisabolol): Volatile compounds, such as bisabolol, help relax the smooth muscles in the digestive tract, reducing spasms and alleviating heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are the most commonly used part of Zingiber officinale due to their high concentration of gingerols and shogaols, which have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Roots: Roots of Zingiber officinale are also used to make creams for heartburn due to their gingerol content, which helps to reduce inflammation and provide relief from heartburn.

Stems: While less commonly used, stems of Zingiber officinale may be used in some creams for heartburn due to their ability to aid digestion and reduce inflammation, although their effectiveness is less documented compared to rhyzomes and roots.
remedy_recipe: Gather 30g of dried zingiber officinale roots and 100g of coconut oil for base.
Infuse the zingiber officinale roots in 200ml of carrier oil for 2 hours at 50c.
Filter the infused oil through a cheesecloth to remove zingiber officinale roots.
Combine 100g of the filtered oil with 50g of beeswax in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture constantly for 10 minutes until it reaches 40c to 50c consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with heartburn because they contain the active compound glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Glycyrrhizin works by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating heartburn symptoms. Additionally, the cream's moisturizing properties help to protect the mucous membranes, promoting healing and preventing further irritation. By providing a protective barrier and reducing inflammation, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can provide quick relief from heartburn discomfort and promote overall digestive health.
remedy_constituents: Licorice Saponins: These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which can contribute to heartburn symptoms.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra have antioxidant properties that help to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract, reducing irritation and discomfort associated with heartburn.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra have anti-inflammatory and protective effects on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and preventing damage to the esophagus and stomach lining, which can contribute to heartburn.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Glycyrrhiza glabra are widely used in creams for heartburn due to their high content of glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the digestive system.

Leaves: The leaves of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in creams for heartburn because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have antacid and anti-inflammatory effects, helping to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation.

Seeds: The seeds of Glycyrrhiza glabra are used in creams for heartburn as they contain saponins, which help to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues, providing relief from heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Extract 250 grams of dried root powder of glycyrrhiza glabra in a double boiler for 2 hours.
Mix the extracted root powder with 500 grams of sweet almond oil and 200 grams of beeswax in a heatproof bowl.
Heat the mixture over a double boiler at 60-70 degrees celsius for 30 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container and discard the solids.
Store the herbal cream in a cool dark place for 24 hours before using it for skin care applications.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams helps with heartburn because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The gel extracted from the aloe vera plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin that have been shown to reduce inflammation in the esophagus and ease digestive discomfort. This natural remedy can help calm heartburn symptoms by reducing acid production and promoting the healing of damaged tissue in the esophagus, providing quick relief from heartburn and acid reflux. It also helps to neutralize stomach acid.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Aloin: An anthraquinone glycoside, aloin has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes, providing relief from heartburn and digestive discomfort.
Acemannan: A polysaccharide, acemannan has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help calm the digestive system and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Extracted gel from the leaves of Aloe barbadensis is used to soothe heartburn symptoms due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The gel from the leaves also contains aloin, which helps to reduce inflammation and neutralize stomach acid.
Leaves: The aloe vera leaf gel also contains vitamins, minerals, and enzymes that help to heal and protect the mucous membranes in the esophagus, reducing heartburn symptoms.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100 grams of aloe barbadensis gel and 10 grams of vaseline in a stainless steel container.
Melt 20 grams of beeswax in a double boiler for 5 minutes at 180 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine the melted beeswax with the aloe barbadensis gel and vaseline in the container.
Blend the mixture at 8000 revolutions per minute for 3 minutes using a high-speed blender.
Pour the cream into sterilized containers and let it cool at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare creams helps with heartburn because they contain anethole, a natural compound that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. Anethole helps to relax the muscles in the esophagus and stomach, reducing the frequency and severity of heartburn episodes. Additionally, anethole has a soothing effect on the digestive tract, promoting digestion and reducing inflammation. By applying a Foeniculum vulgare cream to the affected area, individuals can experience relief from heartburn symptoms and promote overall digestive well-being.
remedy_constituents: Terpenes: Terpenes in Foeniculum vulgare have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids in Foeniculum vulgare have antioxidant properties, which can help protect the esophageal mucosa from oxidative stress and damage caused by acid reflux.
Anethole: Anethole in Foeniculum vulgare has a calming effect on the muscles in the digestive tract, which can help reduce spasms and alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.
remedy_parts: Seeds: The seeds of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make creams for heartburn due to their high content of volatile oils, particularly anethole, which provides anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.

Leaves: The leaves of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make creams for heartburn because they contain a high amount of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that help alleviate heartburn symptoms.

Roots: The roots of Foeniculum vulgare are used to make creams for heartburn due to their rich content of essential oils, including anethole, which helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract and reduce inflammation, providing relief from heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh foeniculum vulgare roots and leaves in the morning when dew is still present.
Steep the harvested plant material in 500ml of carrier oil for 2-3 weeks in a cool dark place.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove plant residue after 2-3 weeks.
Mix 10% of the infused oil with 90% of a base cream in a double boiler or a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Allow the herbal cream to cool and harden before transferring it to a clean glass jar for storage.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams helps with heartburn because of its natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. The menthol content in peppermint oil found in these creams helps relax the muscles in the digestive tract, reducing inflammation and providing relief from heartburn symptoms. Additionally, mentha x piperita creams can also help neutralize stomach acid and improve digestion, further alleviating heartburn discomfort. This makes mentha x piperita creams a popular natural remedy for heartburn sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Helps with heartburn by providing rapid relaxation of the lower esophageal sphincter, thereby reducing the symptoms of acid reflux.
Rosmarinic acid: Helps with heartburn by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe and protect the esophagus and stomach lining.
Limonene: Helps with heartburn by exerting anti-inflammatory effects, reducing the inflammation and irritation associated with acid reflux and heartburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Peppermint leaves are the most commonly used part in creams for heartburn due to their high concentration of menthol, which provides quick relief from digestive discomfort.
Leaves: In addition to menthol, peppermint leaves contain menthone, which helps to relax the muscles in the stomach and reduce inflammation, further alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Leaves: The essential oils extracted from peppermint leaves, including menthol and menthone, can also help to reduce acid production in the stomach, providing further relief from heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of fresh mentha x piperita leaves in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Mix 1/2 cup of coconut oil and 1/4 cup of beeswax in a double boiler at 180°f for 10 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of dried mentha x piperita flowers to the coconut oil mixture and infuse for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and combine with 2 tablespoons of aloe vera gel and 1 teaspoon of vitamin e oil.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and refrigerate at 40°f for at least 2 hours before serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Althaea officinalis creams helps with heartburn because they provide relief from inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach lining. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow root, the main ingredient in Althaea officinalis, soothe and protect the mucous membranes, reducing the risk of acid reflux and heartburn. By creating a protective barrier, these creams prevent stomach acid from irritating the esophagus, providing quick and effective relief from heartburn symptoms. This natural remedy offers a gentle, non-invasive solution for heartburn sufferers.
remedy_constituents: Mucilages: These complex carbohydrates help protect and soothe the mucous membranes in the esophagus and stomach, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
Flavonoids: These plant-based compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and damage to the esophageal lining, alleviating heartburn symptoms.

Polysaccharides: These complex sugars can help form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, preventing acid from irritating the esophagus and reducing the frequency and severity of heartburn episodes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to make creams for heartburn because they are rich in mucilage, a soothing gel-like substance that helps to protect and calm the mucous membranes in the esophagus.

Leaves: The leaves are used to make creams for heartburn because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the esophagus.

Flowers: The flowers are used to make creams for heartburn because they have a soothing and anti-inflammatory effect on the mucous membranes, which can help to alleviate symptoms of heartburn and acid reflux.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200 grams of althaea officinalis roots and dry them in a low-temperature oven at 50°c for 4 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 5 minutes.
Steep 100 grams of the ground powder in 1000 milliliters of boiling distilled water for 30 minutes.
Strain the infusion using a cheesecloth and discard the solids to obtain a liquid extract.
Mix the liquid extract with 100 grams of beeswax and 500 milliliters of distilled water in a double boiler to create a smooth cream.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams helps with heartburn because they contain anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that calm the digestive tract and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. Aloe vera's natural gel helps to neutralize stomach acid, promoting a healthy pH balance. Its mucilaginous properties also form a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, shielding them from acid reflux and providing instant relief from heartburn symptoms, allowing for a quick recovery and reduction in discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: This anthraquinone derivative helps with heartburn by soothing the mucous membranes and reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach.
Alizarin: This anthraquinone constituent has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce irritation and inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating heartburn symptoms.
Saponins: These triterpenoid saponins have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which help to calm the mucous membranes and reduce acid reflux, providing relief from heartburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in aloe vera gel, which has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that help to alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Gel (from the leaves): The clear, inner gel of the aloe vera leaf is commonly used in heartburn creams due to its calming effects on the digestive system.

Juice (from the leaves): The juice extracted from the leaves of Aloe vera is used in heartburn creams because of its ability to coat and protect the stomach lining, reducing inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_recipe: Gather 3-5 ounces of pure aloe vera gel from the leaf of the aloe vera plant.
Mix 1/4 cup of coconut oil with 1/4 cup of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 2 tablespoons of beeswax to the coconut oil mixture and melt the combination.
Stir in 2 teaspoons of vitamin e oil and 2 teaspoons of honey into the melted mixture.
Pour the final mixture into a container and let it cool for about 30 minutes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis creams helps with heartburn because they contain quinine, a natural alkaloid that has anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. Quinine works by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and relaxing the muscles that can cause heartburn. Additionally, the creams may also help to improve digestion and reduce acid production in the stomach, providing relief from heartburn symptoms. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate heartburn and other digestive issues. Its effectiveness makes it a popular choice for those seeking a natural solution.
remedy_constituents: Quinine alkaloid: Quinine's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, which may alleviate symptoms of heartburn.
Quinovic acid glycoside: Quinovic acid glycoside has a soothing effect on the digestive system, which can help calm heartburn and acid reflux.
Quinic acid: Quinic acid has antioxidant properties, which can help protect the digestive system from damage caused by free radicals, potentially reducing the severity of heartburn symptoms.
remedy_parts: Barks: Cinchona bark contains quinine and other alkaloids, which are used to treat heartburn and other gastrointestinal issues due to their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: Cinchona leaves also contain alkaloids, including quinine, which helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate heartburn symptoms.
Fruits: Cinchona fruits contain alkaloids that have a soothing effect on the stomach and intestines, making them useful in treating heartburn.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of dried cinchona officinalis bark from a trusted supplier and sift it to remove lumps.
Steep 20g of the sifted cinchona officinalis bark in 200ml of organic coconut oil at 50c for 2 hours.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids.
Combine 50g of beeswax with 50g of shea butter in a double boiler and melt at 60c.
Mix the cooled infused oil with the melted wax mixture and whip until a smooth cream forms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia creams helps with heartburn because they contain soothing compounds that calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the esophagus. The anti-inflammatory properties of lavender help to reduce the frequency and severity of heartburn episodes. Additionally, the natural relaxing effects of lavender also help to reduce stress, which is often a trigger for heartburn. By promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia creams can provide relief from heartburn and promote a sense of overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: This terpene has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and ease muscle spasms in the esophagus and stomach, alleviating heartburn symptoms.

Linalyl acetate: As a terpene, linalyl acetate has a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing stress and anxiety that can trigger heartburn, and may also help relax the muscles in the esophagus.

Caffeic acid: A phenolic compound, caffeic acid has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help protect the esophageal mucosa from acid damage, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with heartburn.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high concentration of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds.
Flowers: Used because they contain a high amount of essential oils, particularly linalool and linalyl acetate, which have soothing properties.
Roots: Used due to their content of iridoid glycosides, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and soothing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1/4 cup of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers when they are in full bloom.
Steep 1/4 cup of the dried flowers in 2 cups of boiling distilled water for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a heat-proof container and discard the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of beeswax pellets and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Combine the cooled herbal infusion with the melted wax and oil mixture and whip until smooth.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ulmus rubra creams helps with heartburn because of its soothing and protective properties. The herb Ulmus rubra, also known as slippery elm, forms a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and irritation in the esophagus and stomach. This helps to alleviate the burning sensation associated with heartburn, providing relief and comfort to individuals experiencing this condition. The cream also helps to heal and calm the digestive tract, promoting a healthy gut.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: They help with heartburn by reducing inflammation in the esophagus and stomach, thereby alleviating symptoms of acid reflux.
Tannins: They help with heartburn by forming a protective barrier on the mucous membranes, preventing acid from irritating the lining of the esophagus and stomach.
Phenolic acids: They help with heartburn by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize stomach acid and reduce inflammation in the esophagus and stomach.
remedy_parts: Barks: They are used because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, which can help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the digestive tract.
Leaves: They are used because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and protect the digestive tract from damage.
Roots: They are used because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which can help reduce inflammation and relax the muscles in the digestive tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 200g of fresh ulmus rubra bark and clean it thoroughly with distilled water.
Infuse the ulmus rubra bark in 500ml of distilled water for 2 hours at 95°c.
Strain the infused mixture through a cheesecloth to remove the solids and discard the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with 50g of beeswax and 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes a smooth, creamy texture after 30 minutes.
